channel.title: Security on RadixArk Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Security on RadixArk Blog

item.title: The Mathematics of RSA Cryptography
item.description:
The backbone of HTTPS and API authentication relies on Public Key Cryptography. Specifically, the RSA algorithm, which leans heavily on the difficulty of factoring the product of two large prime numbers.

Key Generation

  1. Choose two distinct large primes, \( p \) and \( q \).
  2. Compute their product: $$ n = p \cdot q $$
  3. Calculate Euler’s totient function: \[ \varphi(n) = (p - 1)(q - 1) \]
  4. Choose an integer \( e \) (the public exponent) such that \( 1 < e < \varphi(n) \) and \( \gcd(e, \varphi(n)) = 1 \).
  5. Determine \( d \) (the private exponent) as the modular multiplicative inverse of \( e \) modulo \( \varphi(n) \): $$ d \equiv e^{-1} \pmod{\varphi(n)} $$

Encryption and Decryption

A sender encrypts a message \( m \) (represented as an integer) using the public key \( (n, e) \):
